key_insight: A one-step flow matching model in latent space can generate high-resolution path-dependent stress fields, including plastic loading-unloading histories, without multi-step diffusion sampling.
engineering_relevance: This offers a fast surrogate for finite element simulation of complex geometries and constitutive paths, enabling much cheaper field prediction for computational mechanics workflows.
method: Transformer-based flow matching in VAE latent space
Treats stress evolution as video synthesis across all time steps
Uses non-Gaussian source distribution plus token-level loading embeddings and auxiliary networks
evidence_signal: The abstract reports accurate generation on a limited dataset and speedups of 6–7× over FEM on CPUs and about two orders of magnitude on consumer GPUs.
watch_item: Performance may depend on the learned loading-path distribution and geometry coverage, so unseen path-dependent conditions could be harder to generalize.
paper_title: One-Step Flow Matching for Generative Modeling of Path-Dependent Physical Fields
